text:
My implementation plan for the React client is ready for review. Here is a summary:

**File Structure**: 17 files across `src/client/` -- `App.tsx`, 14 components, 4 hooks, 1 CSS file, 1 client-only types file, plus `index.html`.

**Component Tree**: `App` renders `Header` + `MessageList` (scrollable chat column) + sidebar (`TaskSidebar`, `PresenceRoster`, `SessionStats`). MessageList renders `ChatMessage`, `SystemEvent`, `ThreadBlock`, `PlanApprovalCard`, `PermissionRequestCard` with `ReactionRow` beneath target messages. `NewMessageIndicator` floats at bottom.

**Message Rendering**:
- Lead: right-aligned, indigo bubble, crown avatar
- Teammates: left-aligned, gray-800 bubble, colored left accent stripe, colored circle avatar
- System: centered, muted, small text, no bubble
- DMs: collapsible ThreadBlock with "-- A -> B (DM) --" header/footer
- Broadcasts: normal bubble + broadcast icon next to sender
- Plan cards: bordered card, collapsible plan content, approval reactions
- Permission cards: code block for command, status icon
- Reactions: Slack-style pill row below target message

**Task Sidebar**: progress bar, task cards with status icons/owner/duration/blocked-by, pulse animation on unblock, click-to-scroll to related messages.

**Presence Roster**: color dot + agent name + working/idle/offline icon. Lead first with crown.

**Dark Theme**: gray-950 page, gray-900 sidebar, gray-800 teammate bubbles, indigo-600 lead bubbles. Linear/Raycast aesthetic.

**Animations**: 150ms slide-in (left for teammates, right for lead), reaction scale pop-in, smooth thread expand/collapse, 3s pulse on task unblock. All CSS, no JS animation libraries.

**State**: useReducer with ChatState (events array, tasks map, presence map, reactions map). WebSocket dispatches ChatEvents directly. HYDRATE action for initial GET /state.

**Auto-scroll**: track scroll position, auto-scroll if within 100px of bottom, otherwise show floating "New messages" indicator.

**Timestamps**: relative for <1hr, absolute for >=1hr, full ISO on hover.

**Content**: basic markdown (bold, italic, code, lists, @mentions). Long messages truncate at 200 chars with expand. Code blocks collapsible.

Full plan details are in the plan file. Awaiting approval before writing any code. Note: Task #4 is blocked by #1 (shared types) which is still pending.
summary: Client implementation plan ready for review

text: {"type":"idle_notification","from":"client","timestamp":"2026-03-10T01:00:02.120Z","idleReason":"available","summary":"[to server] Blocked on shared types -- requesting status on task #1"}

{"type":"plan_approval_request","from":"server","timestamp":"2026-03-10T01:00:24.128Z","planFilePath":"/Users/brianruggieri/.claude/plans/concurrent-shimmying-garden-agent-a6ebaa15bbcd9f8e3.md","planContent":"# Server Agent Plan: Tasks #1 and #2

## Summary

Task #1 (shared types) must be done first since #2 is blocked by it. I will implement both sequentially:

1. **Task #1**: `src/shared/types.ts` + `src/shared/parse.ts`
2. **Task #2**: `src/server/watcher.ts`, `src/server/processor.ts`, `src/server/journal.ts`, `src/server/server.ts`

After task #1 is complete, I will message "fixtures" and "client" so they can start using the types.

---

## Task #1: Shared Types

### `src/shared/types.ts`

Adopts the types from the handoff doc with these adjustments:
- Add `id` field to `PresenceChange` and `TaskUpdate` (every event needs a unique ID for client keying and journal dedup)
- Add `JournalEntry` type wrapping `ChatEvent` with sequence number for replay
- Add raw filesystem types: `RawInboxMessage`, `RawTaskData`, `TeamConfig`
- Add `ParsedSystemEvent` type for classified inbox system events
- Export all types (both agents import from here)

Types to define:
- `RawInboxMessage` — shape of entries in inbox JSON arrays
- `RawTaskData` — shape of task entries in tasks.json
- `AgentInfo` — member entry from config.json
- `TeamConfig` — config.json shape `{ members: AgentInfo[] }`
- `TeamState` — runtime team state `{ name, members }`
- `TaskInfo` — processed task (same shape as RawTaskData, kept separate for clarity)
- `ChatEvent` — discriminated union: `ContentMessage | SystemEvent | ReactionEvent | ThreadMarker | PresenceChange | TaskUpdate`
- `ContentMessage` — `{ type: 'message', id, from, fromColor, text, summary, timestamp, isBroadcast, isDM, dmParticipants, isLead, replyToId }`
- `SystemEvent` — `{ type: 'system', id, subtype, text, timestamp, agentName, agentColor, taskId, taskSubject }`
- `ReactionEvent` — `{ type: 'reaction', id, targetMessageId, emoji, fromAgent, fromColor, timestamp, tooltip }`
- `ThreadMarker` — `{ type: 'thread-marker', id, subtype: 'thread-start' | 'thread-end', participants, timestamp }`
- `PresenceChange` — `{ type: 'presence', id, agentName, status, timestamp }`
- `TaskUpdate` — `{ type: 'task-update', id, task: TaskInfo, timestamp }`
- `SessionState` — `{ team, events, tasks, presence, sessionStart }` for REST `/state` hydration
- `PlanApprovalCard` — structured data for plan approval UI cards
- `PermissionRequestCard` — structured data for permission request UI cards
- `JournalEntry` — `{ seq: number, event: ChatEvent }`
- `SystemEventType` — string literal union of known system event type values from inbox text field
- `ParsedSystemEvent` — parsed JSON from inbox message text field with known type

### `src/shared/parse.ts`

Parsing utilities:
- `tryParseSystemEvent(text: string): ParsedSystemEvent | null` — JSON.parse the text field, return typed result if it has a known `type`, null for plain content
- `isLeadAgent(name: string): boolean` — returns `name === 'team-lead'`
- `generateEventId(): string` — unique ID via `crypto.randomUUID()`
- `parseTimestamp(ts: string): number` — ISO8601 to epoch ms
- `isWithinWindow(ts1: string, ts2: string, windowMs: number): boolean` — check if two timestamps are within windowMs

---

## Task #2: Server Components

### `src/server/watcher.ts` — File Watcher

Watches three filesystem locations using `fs.watch` (Bun native):
- `~/.claude/teams/{team-name}/inboxes/*.json` — inbox files
- `~/.claude/tasks/{team-name}/tasks.json` or `{id}.json` — task files
- `~/.claude/teams/{team-name}/config.json` — team roster

Design:
```
class FileWatcher:
  - inboxSnapshots: Map<string, RawInboxMessage[]>   // previous content per inbox
  - taskSnapshot: Map<string, RawTaskData>            // previous task states
  - configSnapshot: TeamConfig | null
  - debounceTimers: Map<string, Timer>                // 100ms debounce per path

  constructor(options: WatcherOptions)
  start(): void           // begin watching, handle missing dirs gracefully
  stop(): void            // close all watchers

  callbacks:
    onInboxChange(agentName, newMessages[])   // only NEW messages since last read
    onTasksChange(tasks[])                     // full current task list
    onConfigChange(config)                     // full current config
```

Key behaviors:
- **100ms debounce** per file path — agents write rapidly, avoid processing partial JSON
- **try/catch on JSON.parse** — files can be mid-write, skip silently
- **Diff against snapshots** — inbox arrays grow monotonically, emit only new entries
- **Handle missing files** — inboxes created lazily, don't error
- **Watch for new files** — detect new inbox files appearing (new agent joins)
- Support both `tasks.json` (single file with array) and individual `{id}.json` files

### `src/server/processor.ts` — Event Processor

Core logic transforming raw filesystem changes into ChatEvents.

```
class EventProcessor:
  // State
  events: ChatEvent[]
  presence: Map<string, { status, since }>
  tasks: Map<string, TaskInfo>
  members: Map<string, AgentInfo>
  messageIndex: Map<string, ContentMessage>     // for reaction targeting

  // Broadcast detection
  holdBuffer: Map<string, { msg, inboxes: Set, timer }>
  // Key = hash(text + rounded_timestamp), hold 500ms

  // Idle suppression
  idleTimers: Map<string, Timer>                // agent -> 30s surface timer
  idlePingCount: Map<string, number>

  // Callbacks
  onEvent: (event: ChatEvent) => void           // called for each produced event

  // Entry points
  processInboxChange(inboxOwner: string, newMessages: RawInboxMessage[]): void
  processTasksChange(tasks: RawTaskData[]): void
  processConfigChange(config: TeamConfig): void
  getState(): SessionState
```

**Message classification flow** (per new inbox message):
1. Call `tryParseSystemEvent(text)`
2. If null (plain content):
   - Hash text+timestamp, check holdBuffer for broadcast detection
   - If hash exists: add this inbox to the set, if 3+ inboxes -> mark as broadcast
   - If hash is new: start 500ms timer, add to holdBuffer
   - When timer fires: emit ContentMessage (isBroadcast = inboxes.size >= 3)
   - Deduplicate: only emit ONE ContentMessage for broadcasts, not N
   - Check DM: inbox owner != team-lead AND from != team-lead -> isDM=true
   - Track DM pairs for ThreadMarker start/end (5min gap = thread end)
3. If system event:
   - `idle_notification`: update presence to 'idle', emit PresenceChange; start 30s timer, if fires emit SystemEvent 'idle-surfaced'; reset timer on each new ping
   - `shutdown_request`: emit SystemEvent 'shutdown-requested'
   - `shutdown_approved`: emit SystemEvent 'shutdown-approved' + PresenceChange 'offline'
   - `shutdown_rejected`: emit SystemEvent 'shutdown-rejected'
   - `task_completed`: emit SystemEvent 'task-completed'; check all-tasks-completed
   - `plan_approval_request`: emit ContentMessage with plan card data in text (client parses)
   - `plan_approval_response`: emit ReactionEvent (thumbsup/thumbsdown) on the plan message
   - `permission_request`: emit ContentMessage with permission card data
   - `join_request`: emit SystemEvent 'member-joined' (hold until config confirms)
   - Any non-idle message from agent: cancel idle timer, set presence 'working'

**Broadcast detection** (500ms hold window):
- Key = `text.slice(0,100) + '|' + Math.round(parseTimestamp(ts) / 1000)` (1s tolerance)
- On first appearance: store in holdBuffer with 500ms setTimeout
- On subsequent: add inbox to the set
- Timer fires: emit event; if inboxes.size >= 3, isBroadcast=true; pick one canonical message

**Idle suppression** (30s threshold):
- Each idle_notification: emit PresenceChange 'idle' (sidebar update only)
- Start/reset a 30s timer per agent
- If timer fires: emit SystemEvent 'idle-surfaced' (one chat message)
- Any non-idle activity from that agent: cancel timer, emit PresenceChange 'working'

**Task claim correlation** (120s scan window):
- On TaskUpdate where owner changes null -> agentName:
- Scan events backwards for ContentMessages from 'team-lead' in last 120s
- Match by task subject substring or `#taskId` reference
- If found: emit ReactionEvent with emoji '✋' targeting that message
- Always emit SystemEvent 'task-claimed'

**Dependency unblock cascades**:
- On any task status -> 'completed':
- For each task with blockedBy array:
  - Check if ALL blockedBy IDs now have 'completed' status
  - If newly unblocked (wasn't before): emit SystemEvent 'task-unblocked'
- After processing: if ALL tasks are 'completed' -> emit SystemEvent 'all-tasks-completed'

### `src/server/journal.ts` — JSONL Journal

```
class Journal:
  filePath: string            // ~/.teamchat/sessions/{team-name}.jsonl
  seq: number                 // monotonic counter

  constructor(teamName: string, sessionsDir?: string)
  append(event: ChatEvent): void     // write JSON line + newline, flush
  static read(filePath: string): JournalEntry[]           // read all for replay
  static async *stream(filePath: string): AsyncGenerator<JournalEntry>  // for large files
```

- Creates `~/.teamchat/sessions/` directory if it doesn't exist
- Each line: `JSON.stringify({ seq, event })` + `\n`
- Append-only, `Bun.write` with append flag

### `src/server/server.ts` — HTTP + WebSocket Server

```
class TeamchatServer:
  server: ReturnType<typeof Bun.serve>
  clients: Set<ServerWebSocket>
  processor: EventProcessor
  journal: Journal | null

  constructor(options: ServerOptions)
  start(): void
  stop(): void
  broadcast(event: ChatEvent): void    // send to all WS clients + journal

  Routes:
    GET /          -> serve index.html (from client build dir)
    GET /state     -> JSON response with SessionState
    GET /assets/*  -> serve static files
    WS /ws         -> upgrade, push ChatEvents as JSON
```

- `Bun.serve()` with `fetch` handler for HTTP and `websocket` handler
- On WS open: no automatic state push (client fetches /state via REST)
- On each ChatEvent from processor: `JSON.stringify(event)` to all WS clients
- If journal enabled: append to journal on each event
- Port defaults to 3456, configurable

---

## Execution Order

1. Create directory structure: `src/shared/`, `src/server/`, `bin/`
2. Write `src/shared/types.ts`
3. Write `src/shared/parse.ts`
4. Mark task #1 complete, message fixtures and client agents
5. Write `src/server/watcher.ts`
6. Write `src/server/processor.ts`
7. Write `src/server/journal.ts`
8. Write `src/server/server.ts`
9. Mark task #2 complete, message fixtures agent for integration tests
","requestId":"plan_approval-1773104424128@server@teamchat-build"}

Here is my implementation plan for tasks #6, #7, and #8. Requesting approval before I start coding.

## Task #6: Fixture Generator (`fixtures/generate.ts`)

**Output structure:**
```
fixtures/output/healthdash-sprint/
  teams/healthdash-sprint/
    config.json                  # 5 members (team-lead, backend, frontend, privacy, qa)
    inboxes/
      team-lead.json             # ~25 entries (content msgs, plan approval, permission requests, idle notifications, shutdown approvals)
      backend.json               # ~9 entries (broadcast, DMs from qa/frontend, shutdown request)
      frontend.json              # ~10 entries (broadcast, DMs from backend/privacy, shutdown request)
      privacy.json               # ~12 entries (broadcast, DMs from frontend, plan approval response, shutdown request)
      qa.json                    # ~7 entries (broadcast, DM from backend, shutdown request)
  tasks/healthdash-sprint/
    tasks.json                   # 8 tasks, all completed in final state
```

**Code layout:**
- `fixtures/data/config.ts` -- team member definitions (5 members with distinct colors: gold, blue, green, purple, yellow)
- `fixtures/data/tasks.ts` -- 8 task definitions with dependency chains + array of 17 task state transitions for replay
- `fixtures/data/events.ts` -- every transcript event as typed objects (timestamp, from, to[], text, summary, systemEvent)
- `fixtures/generate.ts` -- main script that routes events to inbox files and writes everything to disk. Accepts `--output <dir>`.

**Broadcast messages (same text in 4+ inboxes):**
1. Lead's assignment (10:00:31)
2. Backend's API contract (10:28:44)
3. Privacy's PHI cache finding (11:15:30)
4. Lead's wrap-up (11:23:10)

**DM messages (teammate-to-teammate):**
1. backend -> frontend+privacy schema (10:05:42)
2. frontend -> backend acknowledgment (10:05:58)
3. frontend <-> privacy masking thread (10:21:17-10:22:00, 3 messages)
4. qa <-> backend RBAC bug thread (10:49:30-10:49:50, 2 messages)

**System events in inboxes:**
- plan_approval_request (privacy, 10:08:33) + plan_approval_response (10:09:01)
- permission_request x2 (qa, 10:14:22 and 11:04:33)
- idle_notification (privacy 10:55:18, frontend 10:58:41)
- shutdown_request x4 + shutdown_approved x4 (11:23:22)

## Task #7: Package Config + README

**package.json:** name "teamchat", bin pointing to `./bin/teamchat.ts`, scripts (dev, build, test, fixture:generate), chokidar as only runtime dep, React/Tailwind/bun-types as devDeps, ESM module.

**tsconfig.json:** strict mode, ESNext target/module, bundler moduleResolution, react-jsx, bun-types, includes src/, bin/, fixtures/.

**README.md:** What it is, quick start (`npx teamchat --team my-team`), auto-launch hook config, CLI reference table, how it works (filesystem watching), replay mode, development setup, MIT license.

## Task #8: Integration Tests (blocked by #2 + #6)

Six test files using Bun test runner:
- `processor.test.ts` -- message ordering, all-tasks-completed detection
- `broadcast.test.ts` -- broadcast detection (4 broadcasts) + non-broadcast negative cases
- `dm-threads.test.ts` -- DM thread grouping for 3 thread blocks
- `idle-suppression.test.ts` -- QA's 42min idle suppressed, meaningful idles surfaced
- `task-cascade.test.ts` -- dependency unblock cascades (#1 -> unblocks #2/#3/#4, #6 fully unblocks when #2+#3+#4 done, etc.)
- `reactions.test.ts` -- task claim reactions, plan approval reactions, all-tasks-completed celebration

Tests import the processor from `src/server/processor.ts` and feed it fixture data chronologically.

## Execution Order
1. Start #6 and #7 in parallel immediately
2. Wait for server agent's processor (#2) + my #6 to complete, then write #8

## Dependency Note
I need `src/shared/types.ts` to exist before my TypeScript compiles. I can start writing my code now and it will compile once the server agent writes types.ts. My fixture generator writes raw JSON so it has no runtime dependency on other agents' code.

Ready to proceed on your approval.
summary: Implementation plan for fixtures, config, and tests

text:
{"type":"plan_approval_request","from":"server","timestamp":"2026-03-10T01:01:11.931Z","planFilePath":"/Users/brianruggieri/.claude/plans/concurrent-shimmying-garden-agent-a20c1326b519234fc.md","planContent":"# Server Agent — Implementation Plan (Tasks #1, #2, #3)

All three tasks assigned. Executing sequentially since each depends on the prior.

## Task #1: Shared Types (src/shared/types.ts + src/shared/parse.ts)

Create directory structure, then write:

**types.ts**: All shared type definitions per handoff doc — RawInboxMessage, RawTaskData, AgentInfo, TeamConfig, TeamState, TaskInfo, ChatEvent discriminated union (ContentMessage, SystemEvent, ReactionEvent, ThreadMarker, PresenceChange, TaskUpdate), SessionState, PlanApprovalCard, PermissionRequestCard, JournalEntry, SystemEventType, ParsedSystemEvent. Add `id` field to PresenceChange and TaskUpdate for client keying.

**parse.ts**: tryParseSystemEvent, isLeadAgent, generateEventId, parseTimestamp, isWithinWindow utilities.

After completion: message fixtures + client agents, mark task #1 done.

## Task #2: Server Components

**watcher.ts**: FileWatcher class using fs.watch with 100ms debounce per path, try/catch JSON.parse, snapshot diffing for inbox arrays (emit only new messages), graceful missing file handling.

**processor.ts**: EventProcessor class — message classification via tryParseSystemEvent, broadcast detection (500ms hold window, hash text+rounded timestamp, emit once if 3+ inboxes), idle suppression (PresenceChange on each ping, 30s timer for chat surface), DM detection (non-lead inbox + non-lead sender), task claim correlation (120s backward scan for lead messages referencing task), dependency unblock cascades (check all blockedBy on completion), all-tasks-completed detection, thread markers for DM conversation grouping.

**journal.ts**: Journal class — append ChatEvents as JSONL to ~/.teamchat/sessions/{team-name}.jsonl, static read/stream methods for replay.

**server.ts**: TeamchatServer class using Bun.serve — GET / serves client, GET /state returns SessionState, WS /ws pushes ChatEvents, broadcast to all connected clients.

After completion: message fixtures agent, mark task #2 done.

## Task #3: CLI Entry Point

**bin/teamchat.ts**: Parse args (--team, --watch, --replay, --port, --compact, --no-journal, --share, setup). Wire FileWatcher + EventProcessor + Journal + TeamchatServer. Replay mode reads JSONL and serves with timestamp-based playback at configurable speed.

After completion: mark task #3 done.

{"type":"plan_approval_request","from":"client","timestamp":"2026-03-10T01:02:08.944Z","planFilePath":"/Users/brianruggieri/.claude/plans/concurrent-shimmying-garden-agent-a22ce551a82ca2334.md","planContent":"# Client Agent Implementation Plan

## Status
- Task #4 (Scaffold React client components) -- blocked by #1 (shared types, server working on it)
- Task #5 (Implement full React client with styling) -- blocked by #4

Once #1 lands, I execute #4 then #5 sequentially.

---

## File Structure

```
src/client/
  index.html              -- SPA entry point, loads bundled JS + Tailwind CDN
  App.tsx                  -- Root component: WebSocket + GET /state, useReducer, layout shell
  types.ts                 -- Client-only types (ChatState, reducer actions) -- NOT shared types
  components/
    ChatMessage.tsx         -- Content message bubble (left/right alignment, markdown, expand/collapse)
    SystemEvent.tsx         -- Centered muted system event line
    ThreadBlock.tsx         -- Collapsible DM thread container with header/footer markers
    ReactionRow.tsx         -- Slack-style reaction row below a message
    PlanApprovalCard.tsx    -- Expandable plan card with approval status + reactions
    PermissionRequestCard.tsx -- Tool/command permission card with status
    TaskSidebar.tsx         -- Right sidebar: progress bar, task list, click-to-scroll
    TaskCard.tsx            -- Individual task item in sidebar
    PresenceRoster.tsx      -- Agent list with color dot + status icon
    SessionStats.tsx        -- Footer: duration, message count, DM threads, task progress
    NewMessageIndicator.tsx -- Floating "New messages" button when not auto-scrolling
    AgentAvatar.tsx         -- First-letter circle avatar, lead gets crown overlay
    MessageList.tsx         -- Scrollable message container, handles auto-scroll logic
    Header.tsx              -- Top bar: teamchat logo, team name, online count
  hooks/
    useWebSocket.ts         -- WebSocket connection + reconnect + dispatch ChatEvents
    useChatReducer.ts       -- useReducer with ChatState + ChatEvent dispatch logic
    useAutoScroll.ts        -- Scroll-to-bottom detection (100px threshold) + indicator state
    useRelativeTime.ts      -- Relative/absolute timestamp formatting with hover ISO
  styles/
    index.css               -- Tailwind base/components/utilities + custom dark theme tokens
```

---

## Component Tree

```
<App>
  <Header teamName={...} onlineCount={...} />
  <main className="flex">
    <MessageList>                          // flex-1, scrollable
      {events.map(event => {
        switch(event.type) {
          'message'       -> <ChatMessage />
          'system'        -> <SystemEvent />
          'thread-marker' -> <ThreadBlock>   // wraps messages between start/end
          'plan-approval' -> <PlanApprovalCard />
          'permission'    -> <PermissionRequestCard />
        }
        // ReactionRow rendered below target messages
      })}
      <NewMessageIndicator />              // absolute positioned, bottom of list
    </MessageList>
    <aside>                                // w-80, fixed right sidebar
      <TaskSidebar tasks={...} onTaskClick={scrollToTask} />
      <PresenceRoster members={...} presence={...} onAgentClick={filterByAgent} />
      <SessionStats stats={...} />
    </aside>
  </main>
</App>
```

---

## Message Rendering Rules

### Lead messages (right-aligned)
- `isLead: true` on ContentMessage
- Right-aligned with accent background (indigo-600/700)
- Crown avatar on the right side
- Bubble: rounded-l-xl rounded-tr-xl

### Teammate messages (left-aligned)
- `isLead: false` on ContentMessage
- Left-aligned, subtle dark bubble (gray-800)
- Left color accent stripe using `fromColor`
- Colored circle avatar on the left, agent name + color dot above bubble

### System events (centered)
- Centered, muted text (text-gray-500), small font, no bubble
- Grouped if multiple arrive within 5 seconds

### DM threads (collapsible)
- ThreadBlock wraps messages between thread-start and thread-end markers
- Header: "-- sender -> recipient(s) (DM) --" divider
- Footer: "-- back to #general --" divider
- Collapsible with smooth height transition (200ms ease)
- Collapsed shows participant names + message count

### Broadcasts
- Normal bubble + broadcast icon next to sender name

### Plan approval cards
- Bordered card with "PLAN" label + agent name
- Plan content collapsible if >6 lines
- Approval reactions (thumbs-up/down)

### Permission request cards
- "PERMISSION REQUEST" header + agent name
- Tool name + command in code block
- Status icon (pending/approved/denied)

### Reactions
- Small row below target message (matched by targetMessageId)
- Slack-style rounded pill per reaction: emoji + agent name
- Pop-in animation (scale 0->1.1->1, 200ms)

---

## Task Sidebar
- Progress bar: "N/M tasks completed"
- Task cards: status icon + #id + subject + owner + duration + blocked-by
- Pulse animation on unblock (3 seconds)
- Click-to-scroll to related messages

## Presence Roster
- Color dot + agent name + status icon (working/idle/offline)
- Lead first with crown

## Session Stats Footer
- Duration | Messages | DM threads | Tasks progress

---

## Auto-Scroll Logic
- Track scroll position, auto-scroll if within 100px of bottom
- Otherwise show floating "New messages" indicator
- Click indicator to scroll to bottom

## Dark Theme
- bg-gray-950 page, bg-gray-900 sidebar, bg-gray-800 teammate bubbles, bg-indigo-600 lead bubbles
- text-gray-100 primary, text-gray-400 secondary, text-gray-500 timestamps
- Agent colors mapped to Tailwind color classes

## Animations
- Message slide-in: 150ms ease-out (translateX +/-20px -> 0)
- Reaction pop-in: scale bounce 200ms
- Thread expand/collapse: max-height 200ms
- Task unblock pulse: ring animation 3s
- All CSS transitions, no JS animation libraries

## State Management (useChatReducer)
- useReducer with ChatState: events[], tasks Map, presence Map, reactions Map, team, connected
- Reducer handles: message, system, reaction, thread-marker, presence, task-update, HYDRATE

## WebSocket (useWebSocket)
- Connect to ws://localhost:3456/ws
- On open: GET /state for hydration
- On message: dispatch ChatEvent to reducer
- Reconnect with exponential backoff (1s->30s max)

## Content Formatting
- Basic markdown: bold, italic, code, code blocks, lists, @mentions
- Long messages: truncate at 200 chars, expand on click
- Code blocks: collapsible

## Timestamps
- <1hr: relative ("2m ago"), >=1hr: absolute ("10:35 AM"), hover: full ISO

---

## Implementation Order

### Task #4 -- Scaffold
1. Create index.html
2. Create types.ts (client-only types)
3. Create all component files as stubs
4. Create all hook files as stubs
5. Create styles/index.css with Tailwind directives
6. Create App.tsx with layout and imports
7. Verify imports from src/shared/types.ts

### Task #5 -- Full Implementation
1. useChatReducer.ts
2. useWebSocket.ts
3. useAutoScroll.ts
4. useRelativeTime.ts
5. AgentAvatar.tsx
6. ChatMessage.tsx (with markdown + expand/collapse)
7. SystemEvent.tsx
8. ReactionRow.tsx
9. ThreadBlock.tsx
10. PlanApprovalCard.tsx
11. PermissionRequestCard.tsx
12. TaskSidebar.tsx + TaskCard.tsx
13. PresenceRoster.tsx
14. SessionStats.tsx
15. NewMessageIndicator.tsx
16. Header.tsx
17. MessageList.tsx
18. App.tsx (wire everything)
19. styles/index.css (dark theme + animations)
20. Message server agent to confirm WebSocket format

Tasks #6 and #7 are complete. Here's what was built:

**Task #7 — Package Config:**
- `package.json`: name "teamchat", bin entry, scripts (dev/build/test/fixture:generate), React + Tailwind devDeps, chokidar optional
- `tsconfig.json`: strict, ESNext, bundler moduleResolution, react-jsx, bun-types, path aliases
- `README.md`: full CLI reference, auto-launch hook config, how it works, replay mode, dev setup
- `.gitignore`: updated with fixtures/output/, *.tsbuildinfo, .DS_Store

**Task #6 — Fixture Generator:**
- `fixtures/data/config.ts`: 5 members (team-lead gold, backend blue, frontend green, privacy purple, qa yellow) using shared `AgentInfo` type
- `fixtures/data/tasks.ts`: 8 tasks with full dependency chains + 17 task state snapshots using shared `RawTaskData` type
- `fixtures/data/events.ts`: 43 fixture events covering all message types from the transcript:
  - 4 broadcasts (lead assignment, API contract, PHI cache finding, wrap-up)
  - 7 DM messages across 3 threads (backend→frontend+privacy schema, frontend↔privacy masking, qa↔backend RBAC)
  - 2 plan events (privacy plan_approval_request, lead plan_approval_response)
  - 2 permission requests (Playwright install, test run coverage)
  - 8 shutdown events (4 requests + 4 approvals, out-of-order)
  - 20 content messages
- `fixtures/generate.ts`: writes config.json, inboxes/*.json, tasks.json, tasks-initial.json, tasks-snapshots.json

Generator runs successfully: 58 inbox message writes across 5 inboxes.

All fixture types import from `src/shared/types.ts` — no duplicate type definitions.

Ready for Task #8 (integration tests). I need to check that the server processor.ts is ready to test against.

text: Heads up: the tsconfig.json uses `moduleResolution: "bundler"` without `allowImportingTsExtensions`, so import paths need to use `.js` extensions instead of `.ts`/`.tsx`. For example: `import type { ChatEvent } from '../shared/types.js'` instead of `'../shared/types.ts'`. All src/shared/ and src/server/ files use `.js` extensions already. Please update your imports to match.

Task #8 (integration tests) is complete. All 43 tests pass, 3 are marked as todo.

**Test files written (6 total):**
- `fixtures/tests/processor.test.ts` — 7 tests: config creation, task creation, full session replay, getTasks, getPresence, config deletion, member removal
- `fixtures/tests/broadcast.test.ts` — 6 tests: lead broadcasts detected/deduplicated, teammate broadcasts split into DMs + lead copy, single-inbox non-broadcast, DM immediate emit, lead single-recipient non-broadcast
- `fixtures/tests/dm-threads.test.ts` — 7 tests: 4 DM thread pairs, backend→frontend DM, thread-start markers, no duplicate thread-start, qa→backend RBAC DM, lead→teammate NOT DM, teammate→lead NOT DM
- `fixtures/tests/idle-suppression.test.ts` — 6 tests: first ping presence change, pings within 30s suppressed, 30s threshold surfaces event, only one surface per idle period, content message clears idle, multiple simultaneous idle agents
- `fixtures/tests/task-cascade.test.ts` — 11 tests (8 pass, 3 todo): task claims, completions, multiple claims, all-tasks-completed with celebration reaction, task-update events, getTasks state, full session completion
- `fixtures/tests/reactions.test.ts` — 9 tests: plan request renders, plan approval produces thumbs-up, permission request renders, shutdown events, shutdown sets offline, task claim hand-raise correlation, compact mode acks→reactions, non-compact mode normal messages

**Found processor bug:** `computeUnblocks()` in `src/server/processor.ts` never fires because `this.previousTasks` is updated to `currMap` on line 574 BEFORE `computeUnblocks(currMap)` is called on line 577. The unblock detection at line 626-633 compares current state against itself, so it always thinks blockers were already complete and skips. The fix is to move line 574 after line 577 (swap the order of previousTasks update and computeUnblocks call). I've documented this with 3 `test.todo()` entries.

All my tasks (#6, #7, #8) are complete.
